finding: register_office_bridge() calls ziee::chat_extension::register_chat_extension() from the POST-server-start hook (backend/mod.rs:348, after 'server started'), but auto_register_extensions() builds the ExtensionRegistry as a one-time boot SNAPSHOT during chat-module init (chat/mod.rs:61) which runs INSIDE start_server_with_routes — i.e. BEFORE register_office_bridge. So runtime_chat_extensions() is empty when the snapshot is taken and the office_bridge chat extension is never registered.

auto_register_extensions merges runtime_chat_extensions() into a snapshot at call time; because office_bridge registers its extension only post-server-start, the attach_office_bridge_mcp flag is never set on chats, so auto_attach_builtin_ids never attaches the office server either — the entire chat integration silently no-ops. Fix (phase 7): register the office_bridge chat extension + auto-attach entry BEFORE start_server_with_routes builds the chat module (both register_* calls are pool-free static pushes), or make the ExtensionRegistry consult runtime_chat_extensions() dynamically.

TEST-5 exercises runtime_chat_extensions()/the auto-attach registry directly (post-registration), so it does NOT cover the real boot ORDERING — i.e. no test proves the office extension is present in the ExtensionRegistry actually used by chat after a genuine ziee-desktop boot. This coverage gap is exactly why the concurrency bug above slipped through. Add a boot-order assertion in phase 7 alongside the fix.
register_auto_attach_builtin / auto_attach_registry use RwLock with unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) poison-recovery — matches the sync registry pattern; no panic on a poisoned lock. Clean.

runtime_extensions()/register_chat_extension mirror code_sandbox::register_sandbox_mount_provider (OnceLock<RwLock<Vec>> + pub fn) exactly — the proven desktop->server runtime-seam idiom. Conforms to DEC-1/DEC-2.

office_bridge routes/types correctly removed from the web (ui) spec and retained in the desktop spec; both types_ts_parity + types_ts_parity_desktop reported green. No cross-spec contract broken; SyncEntity::OfficeDocument variant intentionally retained in ziee (drives the TS union). Clean.

Repos office_bridge factory field removed; cargo check -p ziee green with ZERO office_bridge; cfg(windows) COM gates moved intact; desktop build.rs picks up the 2 relocated migrations for sqlx verification. Server carries no office_bridge. Clean.

Bridge TLS (rcgen CA+leaf), loopback dual-stack bind, session-token auth, Origin allowlist, and include_dir! add-in assets moved unchanged into the desktop crate — no security posture change vs the audited original. include_dir! base repathed to the desktop crate. Clean.

office_bridge::use grant moved to the desktop 1000-space migration (runs after all server migrations; idempotent array_append; RAISE WARNING if group absent). PermissionCheck impls moved with the module; the auto-attach entry is NOT approval-bypassed (absent from is_builtin_server_id) so mutating office tools stay behind per-call approval (DEC-9). Clean.

OfficeBridge store + sync subscription moved intact to desktop/ui; the Stores.Chat.$ / direct-action API usage (fixed during the main merge) is preserved. glob-driven desktop-loader discovery replaces registry edits (DRIFT-1.2). Clean.

Panel + tool-result card components moved unchanged (kit components, semantic tokens, aria-hidden on decorative icons, logical-direction ps-* all preserved). No a11y regression from the relocation. Clean.

No orphaned office_bridge CODE refs remain in the server — only the intentional OfficeBridgeConfig section (DEC-13) + facade doc-comments. Minor: the config comment cites 'DEC-12' and the PLAN.md intro prose (lines 9-14) still describes the abandoned linkme cross-crate mechanism rather than the runtime seam — stale docs, non-functional. Optional cleanup.
